summary: Average‑quality recording of an unknown‑date show highlighted by a Jimmy Reed cover and an interesting segue from Supplication to Easu.
review: The sole review notes that the band slipped into a Jimmy Reed tune (“you got me running” actually being “Baby What You Want Me To Do”) and calls out an intriguing transition from Supplication into what is labeled “Easu.” No specific comments about sound clarity or mix are provided, leaving the recording quality uncertain while the setlist detail is the main point of interest.

ai_rating.rationale: Only one moderate (3‑star) review with limited details; it mentions a notable song segue but gives no insight into audio quality, leading to an average overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No specific comments on sound clarity, mix balance, or source type; recording quality cannot be reliably assessed.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewer highlights an interesting segue from Supplication to Easu and a surprise Jimmy Reed cover within the set.
